channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Medicaid portals down in all 50 states: US senator
item.description: Portals for Medicaid recipients in all 50 states are down after President Donald Trump ordered a federal spending freeze
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Fewer than half in new poll trust Trump, RFK Jr. to make right recommendations on health issues
item.description: Fewer than half of Americans in a new poll say they trust President Trump or his health-related nominees to make recommendations on the nation's pressing health care issues.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Republicans recommend U.S. marshal, U.S. attorney candidates
item.description: The recommendations from Minnesota’s Republican congressional delegation include former Minneapolis police union leader Bob Kroll for U.S. marshal. For U.S attorney, they include Erica MacDonald, who held the position during Trump’s first term.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Colombia president welcomes first flights of deported migrants after losing public spat with Trump
item.description: Colombia welcomed its first flights of deported illegal immigrants with its president heralding their return and insisting they are not criminals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate confirms Trump pick Sean Duffy for Transportation secretary
item.description: Former Rep. Sean Duffy told lawmakers that his top priorities leading the Department of Transportation include aviation and highway safety.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some Trump voters are skeptical of his opening moves to embrace fellow billionaires
item.description: MESA, Ariz. (AP) — Enrique Lopez votes sporadically but bought into Donald Trump’s vows to fight for everyday workers, helping the Republican flip Arizona last year. Then the home construction contractor watched how the billionaire president opened his second administration.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, BILL BARROW and AMELIA THOMSON DeVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Donald Trump and Project 2025 previewed the federal grant freeze
item.description: A sweeping White House order to freeze federal grants and allocations reflects a theory of presidential power that Donald Trump clearly endorsed during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: HHS will reevaluate programs, regulations to ensure taxpayer funds are not paying for elective abortions
item.description: HHS announced plans to reevaluate agency practices to ensure they meet requirements under the Hyde Amendment, which prohibits federal funds from being used for elective abortions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live updates: White House press briefing tries to add clarity to funding freeze
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt fielded a host of questions from the press corps from the briefing room for the first time on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House press briefing discusses federal assistance, deportation
item.description: Karoline Leavitt addressed the media from the White House's briefing room for the first time on Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s nominee for Pentagon weapons buyer was a key figure in the president's first impeachment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's nominee to be the military's top weapons buyer is an official who directed the Pentagon to withhold aid from Ukraine in 2019 as Trump sought a commitment from President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to investigate the Biden family — a key component of the impeachment
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of former intel officials urge senators to confirm Tulsi Gabbard as director of national intelligence
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Dozens of top former intelligence officials are urging members of the Senate to confirm President Trump’s nominee for Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, saying she will “begin undoing the gross politicization that has come to characterize intelligence bureaucracies," Fo
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump orders a funding freeze as his administration reviews federal loans and grants
item.description: The White House is pausing federal grants and loans starting on Tuesday as President Donald Trump's administration begins an across-the-board ideological review of its spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs an order to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order directing Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth to revise the Pentagon’s policy on transgender troops, likely setting in motion a future ban on their military service.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legal battle is joined as Trump orders a funding freeze during a review of federal loans and grants
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in several states have announced a lawsuit asking a judge to block an imminent federal funding freeze by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Duffy nomination to run Transportation Department
item.description: Former Rep. Sean Duffy (R-Wis.) moved a step closer to becoming the next Transportation secretary as the Senate voted unanimously to tee up a final vote to confirm him. Senators voted 97-0 to invoke cloture on Duffy’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Florida House and Senate leaders take charge in special session showdown
item.description: Florida Governor Ron DeSantis pushed for a special session to help boost President Donald Trump's immigration efforts. But that session was blocked by Senate and House leadership Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Republicans pass Democrats in voter registration, PAC reports
item.description: Nevada Republicans said Monday they have passed Democrats in voter registration in the state for the first time since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Bessent as Trump's Treasury secretary
item.description: Scott Bessent on Monday secured confirmation to lead the Department of the Treasury, putting President Trump’s top economic nominee in place ahead of a daunting GOP push to enact Trump's ambitious economic agenda. Senators voted 68-29 on Bessent’s nomination.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, NewsNation

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff threat
item.description: The White House claimed victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S. on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump threatened various sanctions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Churches have a long history of being safe havens
item.description: For centuries, houses of worship have served as havens for people needing refuge — and, in recent decades, sanctuary from the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: NY Congresswoman Laura Gillen explains Laken Riley Act
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The new U.S. House of Representatives passed its first major, bipartisan legislation this week. The Laken Riley Act requires federal officials to detain undocumented immigrants if they are accused of theft.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump's gender order impacts Californians' passports, driver's licenses
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order declaring the U.S. only recognizes two genders, male and female, has raised questions about what this means for the validity of documents distributed to transgender Californians with their preferred gender marker.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: House passes Laken Riley Act, sending it to Trump for first legislative win
item.description: The House on Wednesday passed the Laken Riley Act, sending the immigration-related bill to President Trump’s desk in what is poised to be his first legislative victory since returning to the White House this week. The chamber cleared the bill in a 263-156 vote.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5
